input: Summarize this thread into exactly 3 bullet points for a standup. Thread: 'Ana: staging is down since the deploy. Ben: rolled back, back up now. Ana: root cause was a bad migration. Ben: writing a fix, ETA tomorrow AM.'
rubric: Award a passing score only if the summary (1) is 3 bullet points, (2) captures the outage + rollback + planned fix, and (3) invents no facts not in the thread. Concise and standup-appropriate.
label: SAMPLE

input: Summarize the key change and the reason in 2 sentences. PR description: 'Switch the cache from in-memory dict to Redis so cache survives restarts and is shared across workers; adds a REDIS_URL env var.'
rubric: Passing only if the summary states BOTH what changed (in-memory cache -> Redis) and why (survives restarts / shared across workers), in at most 2 sentences, with no fabricated details.
label: SAMPLE

input: Rewrite this to be concise and friendly while keeping the Friday 5pm deadline. Draft: 'Hi, I am writing to follow up regarding the report that we discussed, and I would really appreciate it if you could possibly send it over to me by end of day Friday which is 5pm. Thanks so much.'
rubric: Passing only if the rewrite (1) preserves the Friday 5pm deadline, (2) is noticeably more concise than the draft, and (3) stays polite/friendly. Fail if the deadline is dropped or changed.
label: SAMPLE

input: Rewrite this rejection to be kind but clear that the answer is no. Draft: 'We are not moving forward.'
rubric: Passing only if the rewrite clearly communicates a rejection (the answer is no) AND does so with a warm, respectful tone. Fail if it becomes ambiguous about the decision.
label: SAMPLE
